question_structure.context: Cars A and B start moving simultaneously from the same location on a straight, level road. The displacement-time graph of Car A and the velocity-time graph of Car B are shown in Figures A and B, respectively. Find:
question_structure.sub_question_1: After how much time will car B catch up with car A?
question_structure.sub_question_2: What is the maximum distance between the two cars before car B catches up with car A?

sub_question_1.step_1: According to the image, the speed of car A is: $v=\frac{\Delta x}{\Delta t}=10\mathrm{m/s}$
sub_question_1.step_2: The acceleration of car B: $a=\frac{\Delta v}{\Delta t}{=}2\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$
sub_question_1.step_3: Let the time taken for car B to catch up with car A be $t$, then $v t={\frac{1}{2}}a t^{2}$.
sub_question_1.step_4: By simultaneously solving step_1, step_2, and step_3, we obtain: $t=10\mathrm{s}$

sub_question_2.step_5: When the speeds of the two vehicles are equal, the distance is maximized, then $v=a t_{1}$.
sub_question_2.step_8: By simultaneously solving step_5, step_6, and step_7, we obtain: $\Delta x= x_{A} - x_{B}=25\mathrm{m}$

image_captions: The image shows two graphs. Figure A is a position-time (x-t) graph, showing a straight line with a positive slope, passing through the origin and reaching a position of 20m at 2 seconds. Figure B is a velocity-time (v-t) graph, also showing a straight line with a positive slope, starting from the origin and reaching a velocity of 4 m/s at 2 seconds. The time axis (t/s) is the same for both graphs.

step_3.physical_theorem: Displacement of uniform motion and uniform accelerated motion

result_quantity.name: Time taken for car B to catch up with car A

step_8.physical_theorem: Solving simultaneous equations and distance relationship
